description_long: This object is a realistic depiction of a blooming lily plant, featuring two large, open flowers and one closed bud. The flowers display six broad, recurved petals each, colored a soft peach with darker speckles concentrated toward the center. Prominent stamens with elongated, reddish-brown anthers extend outward from the center of each bloom, adding to the intricate detail. The green stem is sturdy and upright, supporting the flowers and several lanceolate leaves that emerge alternately along its length. The leaves are smooth, glossy, and a vibrant green, contrasting with the delicate, patterned petals. The overall structure is elegant and natural, typical of ornamental lilies often found in urban parks or landscaped gardens.
description: A peach-speckled lily plant with two open flowers, a closed bud, and glossy green lanceolate leaves on a single upright stem.
description_view_0: Front view showing both open flowers facing outward, with the closed bud and green leaves clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the profile of one open flower, the closed bud, and the arrangement of leaves along the stem.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the petals, the stem, and the leaves, with one flower partially obscured.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the recurved petals, the closed bud, and the vertical alignment of the stem and leaves.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration with six distinct, recurved petals per flower, overlapping slightly at the base and displaying actinomorphic (radial) symmetry.
